content.heading: Overview
content.body: A 130 sqm apartment for a young professional couple — the brief balanced two working-from-home setups, a desire to entertain regularly, and a clear aesthetic preference for a pared-back, modern interior with personality.

content.caption: The living and dining zone in the evening — a warm palette that transitions from day to night.

items.heading: Working from Home
items.body: Two study zones are built into the apartment without using a dedicated room. One is a full-length desk integrated into the living wall unit; the other is a compact niche carved from a bedroom alcove. Both disappear when not in use.

items.heading: Entertaining
items.body: The dining table extends from four to ten seats. Open kitchen shelving replaces upper cabinets, turning the kitchen into a social backdrop. A bar cabinet in smoked oak acts as a room divider between kitchen and living space.

content.heading: Palette
content.body: Warm white walls, pale timber flooring, terracotta accents, and matte black hardware. A restrained base that makes the art collection — acquired by the clients over years of travel — the visual focus.
